summary.intent: Merge main into the mcp-timeout-config branch, resolve conflicts, then fix two code review issues flagged by Greptile on PR #419.
summary.outcome: Successfully merged main, resolved 5 conflicts in page-mcp.js combining timeout and display_name features, then fixed is_alive logic and renamed effective_request_timeout_secs to configured_request_timeout_secs. All 74 tests pass and changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The mcp-timeout-config branch adds timeout configuration to MCP servers; main added display_name support — both features needed to coexist after merge.
SSE transport is used for both classic SSE and Streamable HTTP servers; is_alive must accept any 2xx response, not just text/event-stream content-type.
effective_request_timeout_secs reflects the configured value (applied on next connect), not the currently-active timeout — naming must reflect this distinction.
The codebase has both crates/mcp and crates/web/src/assets/js layers that share field naming conventions (e.g. configured_request_timeout_secs must match in both Rust and JS).

code.finding: is_alive was rejecting valid Streamable HTTP servers by requiring text/event-stream content-type; fixed to accept any 2xx response.
Renamed effective_request_timeout_secs to configured_request_timeout_secs to clarify it represents the configured value, not the currently active one.
Conflict 1: renderServerName function from main needed to coexist with timeoutVal added to ConfigForm signature from HEAD.
Conflict 2: Both timeoutVal and displayNameVal signals needed to be kept.
Conflicts 3 & 4 (SSE and stdio add paths): addCustomServer pattern with timeout from HEAD merged with display_name from main.
Conflict 5: Both editTimeout and editDisplayName needed to be initialized.

workflow: Greptile automated review on GitHub PRs surfaces semantic issues (naming clarity, logic correctness) worth checking after each push.
After a merge commit+push, a subsequent 'commit and push' request is a no-op — worth confirming state before acting.
Verifying compilation of all dependent crates (mcp + gateway) before committing catches cross-crate breakage from renames.

friction: User issued a redundant 'commit and push' after the merge was already committed — likely unclear whether the previous step had fully completed.
Resolving 5 interleaved conflicts required reading the same large JS file multiple times to gather full context before editing.
Push initially failed due to missing upstream tracking — required a second push command with explicit branch target.
open_items: PR #419 review comments addressed but PR itself not yet merged — further review rounds may follow.
The is_alive fix changes behavior for Streamable HTTP servers; edge cases (non-MCP 2xx endpoints) are not tested.
